creative_brief: Hero image: a real woman's hands opening a brown paper bag on a kitchen bench, with identifiable cuts of beef and farm eggs visible — warm natural light, no supermarket packaging in sight. Below-fold photography of named partner farms (Rachel's Farm cattle on pasture, Collins fishing boat). Earthy palette, no stock photography. Brookvale hub shown as a real place, not a concept render.
headline: "I don't have time to research every piece of meat I buy."
subhead: Neither did a mum from Brookvale. So she stopped researching — and started buying from farmers she could actually name. Here's the moment everything shifted.
hero_copy: She wasn't an activist. She wasn't even a foodie. She was a mum standing in a supermarket aisle, reading a label that said "grass-fed" — on beef that had travelled 2,400km and sat in cold storage for up to 21 days. She thought: there has to be a simpler way to know what I'm feeding my kids. She didn't need more labels. She needed fewer steps between the farm and her kitchen. That's the moment she found FarmThru.

sections.heading: The label said one thing. The supply chain said another.
sections.body:
Here's what she discovered: supermarket beef can spend 14 to 21 days in cold storage. It can travel 2,400km before it reaches the shelf. The packaging says "farm fresh." The reality is feedlots, hormones, antibiotics, and a journey designed to maximise shelf life — not nutrition.

She didn't need a documentary to feel the gap between what she was told and what was true. She just needed one honest look at the chain between paddock and plate. That look changed how she feeds her family.

sections.heading: What if you could skip the entire chain?
sections.body:
FarmThru is a farm-direct grocery store. 100% regenerative farms. Animals raised on pasture. No hormones, no antibiotics, no feedlot finishing. 100% grass-fed beef. Days old, not weeks — straight from their farm to our hub to your kitchen.

You order online. You collect from our Brookvale hub, Monday to Friday. No middlemen inflating the price. No cold chain stretching across the country.

Real farms. Real names. Rachel Ward's regenerative cattle from Kempsey, NSW. Bundarra Farm pork. Collins Wild Caught salmon from the South Coast. Farmer Brown eggs. Paris Creek carbon-neutral bio-dynamic dairy from Meadows, SA. We'll show you where your food comes from — because we actually can.

sections.heading: She didn't just change her shop. She changed her standard.
sections.body:
That's the transformation no one expects. You start by wanting fresher beef. You end up knowing your farmer's name, understanding what regenerative means, and realising your weekly shop is a vote.

Every dollar you spend at FarmThru goes directly to farmers rebuilding soil, raising animals on open pasture, and producing food the way it should be produced. No commitment. Order when you want. Collect from Brookvale Monday to Friday.

This isn't a subscription box. It's a grocery store — built around the radical idea that you deserve to know exactly what you're eating.

sections.heading: Want to own a piece of it?
sections.body:
We're building something that doesn't exist yet — a grocery store where every product traces back to a regenerative farm you can visit. And we're inviting everyday people to be part of it.

Our equity crowdfunding campaign lets you own a piece of FarmThru. Not just shop with us — be a partner in changing how Australia eats.

Place a $5 refundable deposit to join the waitlist. You'll be first to know when the campaign opens. No commitment beyond that.

Investing in early-stage ventures carries real risk, including the possibility of losing your entire investment. This is not a guaranteed return. Only invest what you can afford to lose.

sections.heading: Compliance
sections.body: FarmThru's equity crowdfunding campaign is conducted under the Australian crowd-sourced funding regime (Corporations Act 2001). Investment in early-stage businesses carries significant risk, including the risk of total loss of capital. Past performance is not a reliable indicator of future results. No returns are guaranteed. You should read the offer document in full and consider seeking independent financial advice before making any investment decision. Only invest money you can afford to lose.
